source_quote: Penford and Ingredion executed a Secrecy Agreement, effective as of July 20, 2014, providing for limited exchange of confidential information and obligating the companies to keep their discussions confidential

additional_note: First written indication of interest at $18.00 per share. Board noted it did not reflect full value; process continued.
comments_2: Informal per §G1: filing describes this as an 'indicative valuation' in a letter confirming 'interest in a possible business combination'; non-binding indication, no commitment letters, no process-letter context.

source_quote: On August 10, 2014, Penford received a letter from Mr. Fortnum, confirming Ingredion’s interest in a possible business combination between Ingredion and the Company, citing certain strategic reasons for the proposed acquisition, and providing an indicative valuation of $18.00 per share in cash for all of the outstanding capital stock of Penford on a fully diluted basis.

additional_note: Financial advisor to Penford. Executive Committee decision to retain made July 24; formal engagement letter executed September 11, 2014. Earliest narrated advisory action August 11 (attended Executive Committee meeting reviewing Ingredion proposal).

additional_note: Party A CEO informally discussed potential interest in acquiring or combining with Penford; noted it would base offer on last twelve months performance at a typical takeover premium. No specific price stated.

additional_note: Financial advisor to Ingredion. Earliest narrated advisory action: attendance at August 21 management presentation described as 'Ingredion’s financial advisor.' Date is explicitly stated in the filing.

additional_note: Full nondisclosure and standstill agreement superseding the July 20 Secrecy Agreement. Second NDA event for Ingredion in phase 1.

additional_note: Verbal indication from Fortnum that Ingredion could increase from $18.00 to $18.25-$18.50 range on fully diluted basis. Anchored on receipt date (Sept 17 phone call from Fortnum).

additional_note: Written letter increasing proposed price to $19.00 per share fully diluted. Anchor date: receipt date of letter (October 2, 2014) per §B5.

source_quote: On October 2, 2014, Penford received a letter from Ingredion regarding its indication of interest and increasing the proposed price to $19.00 per share of outstanding Penford capital stock on a fully diluted basis.

additional_note: Verbal indication to Deutsche Bank that any offer would be below $17.50-$18.00 per share. Not yet a formal written bid.
comments_2: Informal per §G1: range bid (structural informal signal, lower < upper); verbally communicated during Deutsche Bank follow-up; described as 'any offer would be below $17.50-$18.00' which is a non-binding range indication without commitment letters.
comments_3: [info:bid_range] Range bid: $17.50 lower, $18.00 upper (single-sided ceiling phrasing 'below $17.50-$18.00')
source_quote: Deutsche Bank followed up with Party A regarding the potential transaction with Penford, pursuant to which Party A indicated any offer would be below $17.50 - $18.00 per share in cash. Deutsche Bank encouraged Party A to submit a letter of interest that could be reviewed by the board of directors.

additional_note: Party D indicated it did not intend to move forward with discussions. Framing is Party D-initiated ('indicated it did not intend') but functionally ends the process; target-side process outcome since Deutsche Bank met with them.
comments_3: [soft:drop_agency_ambiguous] Party D told Deutsche Bank it did not intend to move forward; voluntary withdrawal but framed as communication to target's advisor. Classified DropTarget as process outcome.
source_quote: Deutsche Bank met with representatives from Party D, who indicated that it did not intend to move forward with discussions regarding a potential transaction involving the Company.

additional_note: Party A revised its value range downward from $17.50-$18.00 to $16.00-$18.00 per share, citing increased market volatility.
comments_2: Informal per §G1: range bid (structural informal signal, lower < upper); verbal communication to Deutsche Bank of revised range 'reduced from $17.50-$18.00 to $16.00-$18.00'; non-binding, no commitment letters.
comments_3: [info:bid_range] Range bid: $16.00 lower, $18.00 upper (revised downward from prior range)
source_quote: Deutsche Bank had a discussion with representatives of Party A, who indicated that its value range for a potential transaction had been reduced from $17.50 - $18.00 per share to $16.00 - $18.00 per share, due to increased volatility in the markets.

additional_note: Formal written letter with point-value indication of $16.00 per share. Filing words: 'formal letter with its indication of interest.' Party A cited increased market volatility as reason for decrease.
comments_2: Informal per §G1: filing explicitly calls this 'indication of interest' despite being a 'formal letter'; no commitment letters, no merger agreement markup; price below the $19 deal price and well below the board's stated floor; non-binding indication.
comments_3: NA
source_quote: Party A provided a formal letter with its indication of interest with respect to an acquisition of the Company at a price of $16.00 per share, citing increased volatility in the markets as the reason for the decrease in the offer price.

additional_note: Merger agreement executed evening of October 14, 2014. Press releases issued October 15, 2014. All-cash consideration of $19.00 per share.
